# Verum Compiler (verum_compiler)

The main compiler driver that orchestrates the entire Verum compilation pipeline.

## VBC-First Architecture

The Verum compiler uses a **VBC-first** (Verum Bytecode first) architecture:

```
Source (.vr)
    │
    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  FRONTEND (Phases 0-4)                                       │
│                                                              │
│  Phase 0: stdlib Preparation (embedded VBC)                  │
│  Phase 1: Lexing → Parsing (verum_fast_parser)              │
│  Phase 2: Meta Registry & AST Registration                   │
│  Phase 3: Macro Expansion & Literal Processing               │
│  Phase 3a: Contract Verification (SMT/Z3)                    │
│  Phase 4: Semantic Analysis (Type Inference, CBGR Analysis)  │
│  Phase 4a: Autodiff Compilation                              │
│  Phase 4b: Context System Validation                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    │
    ▼ TypedAST
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  VBC BACKEND (Phases 5-7)                                    │
│                                                              │
│  Phase 5: VBC Code Generation (verum_vbc::codegen)          │
│  Phase 6: VBC Monomorphization (Generic specialization)      │
│  Phase 7: Execution (Two-Tier Model v2.1)                    │
│           ├─ Interpreter (Tier 0, verum_vbc::interpreter)   │
│           └─ AOT (Tier 1, LLVM via verum_codegen)           │
│  Phase 7.5: Final Linking (for AOT)                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    │
    ▼
Executable / Interpreted Result
```

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `pipeline.rs` | Main compilation orchestration + Phase 7 execution |
| `session.rs` | Compilation session state |
| `phases/vbc_codegen.rs` | TypedAST → VBC bytecode generation |
| `phases/vbc_mono.rs` | Generic specialization in VBC |
| `phases/semantic_analysis.rs` | Type checking, CBGR analysis |
| `phases/macro_expansion.rs` | Meta-programming, macros |

> **Note**: VBC execution (Phase 7) is implemented directly in `pipeline.rs` via
> `phase_interpret()` (Tier 0) and `run_native_compilation()` (Tier 1).

## MIR Infrastructure (Not Main Pipeline)

The `phases/mir_lowering.rs` module contains MIR (Mid-level IR) infrastructure
that is **NOT** part of the main compilation pipeline. MIR is used only for:

- SMT-based verification (`phases/verification_phase.rs`)
- Advanced optimization passes (`phases/optimization.rs`)
- CBGR bounds elimination (`passes/cbgr_integration.rs`)

**The actual compilation path goes: TypedAST → VBC → Execution**

## Build Modes

| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `Normal` | User code compilation with pre-loaded stdlib |
| `StdlibBootstrap` | Bootstrap compilation of `core/` library |

## Execution Tiers (v2.1)

| Tier | Mode | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| 0 | `Interpret` | Direct VBC interpretation (fastest startup, ~100ns CBGR) |
| 1 | `Aot` | Ahead-of-time compilation via LLVM (85-95% native speed) |
| - | `Check` | Type checking only, no code generation |

> **Note**: JIT infrastructure exists internally for REPL, incremental compilation,
> and hot reload in dev mode, but it is **NOT** an execution tier.

## Dual-Path Compilation (CPU vs GPU)

MLIR is used **ONLY** for GPU targets. All CPU code uses LLVM IR directly:

```
VBC Bytecode
    │
    ├─── CPU Path (VBC → LLVM IR) ───► Native x86_64/aarch64
    │     • pipeline.rs::run_native_compilation()
    │     • verum_codegen::llvm::VbcToLlvmLowering
    │
    └─── GPU Path (VBC → MLIR) ───► GPU binaries (PTX/HSACO/SPIR-V/Metal)
          • pipeline.rs::run_mlir_aot()
          • verum_codegen::mlir::VbcToMlirGpuLowering
          • Triggered by @device(GPU) annotation or tensor op threshold
```

## Dependencies

- `verum_vbc`: VBC bytecode format, codegen, interpreter
- `verum_types`: Type checking and inference
- `verum_fast_parser`: Fast recursive-descent parser
- `verum_codegen`: Dual-path code generation
  - `llvm/` - CPU: VBC → LLVM IR (inkwell)
  - `mlir/` - GPU: VBC → MLIR → GPU binaries
- `verum_smt`: SMT verification with Z3

## Performance Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Compilation speed | > 50K LOC/sec |
| Type checking | < 100ms / 10K LOC |
| CBGR overhead | < 15ns per check |
| Check elimination | 50-90% (typical) |
